title: Zoto Eval System Cleanup Plan
description: Single-source-of-truth contract for the destructive `cleanup_plan` payload that flows from `/z-eval-configure` (subtask 02 — schema owner) to `scripts/eval-cleanup-stale.ts` (subtask 03 — producer/executor). The configurer diffs the chosen config against the manifest snapshot (`discovery_config.static.framework`, `discovery_config.llm.strategy`, `discovery_config.llm.codeFramework`) and emits this shape. Subtask 03's `--dry-run` stdout MUST match this schema byte-for-byte (same field names, same enums) so the configurer's command-layer `askQuestion` confirmation can render the same plan that the cleanup engine will execute. Compatible with config.schema.json and manifest.schema.json (subtask 01).

schema_version.description: Schema version. Bump this and add a migration when the contract changes.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp at which the configurer (subtask 02) or cleanup engine (subtask 03) emitted the plan.

generated_by.description: Producer of this payload. The configurer command emits the candidate plan; subtask 03's `eval-cleanup-stale --dry-run` re-emits it for execution. Both producers must conform to this schema.

old_snapshot.description: The framework/strategy snapshot read from `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml -> discovery_config` (or filesystem fallback when the snapshot is missing).

new_snapshot.description: The framework/strategy snapshot from the chosen new config (the one the configurer is about to write).

groups.description: Files to delete, grouped by why they are stale. Order is presentation order (the command renders groups in array order during `askQuestion`).

files.description: Total file count across all groups. Must equal the sum of `groups[].files.length`.

groups.description: Number of non-empty groups. Optional convenience for the renderer.

warnings.description: Soft warnings the user should see (e.g. mismatched `static.framework` / `llm.codeFramework` after a strategy switch). Never blocks execution; renders alongside the file list.

snapshot.description: Framework + LLM-strategy snapshot. Fields are optional so legacy manifests (no static/llm block) can still produce a plan via filesystem fallback.

source.description: Where the snapshot values were read from. `manifest` is the canonical source; `filesystem` is the legacy fallback (presence of `evals/conftest.py`, `vitest.config.ts`, `jest.config.{js,ts}`); `config` is the chosen new config; `missing` means no prior snapshot existed (cleanup_plan should be empty).

reason.description: Why every file in this group is stale. `framework-switch`: previous static framework's stamped tests/fingerprints. `strategy-switch`: previous LLM strategy's case files / `*.test.ts` files. `removed-target`: target was added to `config.ignore` or removed from `discoveryTargets`, so its generated eval JSON is now orphaned.

from.description: Previous value (e.g. `pytest`, `declarative`, or the removed target id).

summary.description: One-line human-readable summary used as the askQuestion prompt header for this group.

path.description: Absolute path or repo-relative POSIX path (forward slashes). The cleanup engine resolves repo-relative paths against the repo root.

kind.description: What sort of file this is. `framework-fingerprint`: top-level marker (`evals/conftest.py`, `vitest.config.ts`, `jest.config.{js,ts}`). `static-test`: pytest/vitest/jest test file generated by a static backend. `llm-test`: `*.test.ts` emitted by the LLM `code` strategy. `llm-case`: a single case row in `evals.json` emitted by the LLM `declarative` strategy. `eval-json`: an entire generated `evals.json` file. `directory`: an empty stamped directory to remove after files are deleted. `config-snippet`: a config field/section the cleanup engine should rewrite (rare; always paired with a `summary`).

reason.description: Per-file detail (e.g. `"emitted by templates/static/pytest stamper"`). Optional; the group's `reason`+`summary` cover the common case.

target_id.description: When the file was stamped against a discovered target, the target id (matches manifest `targets[].id`). Lets the cleanup engine cross-check against `manifest.yml` before deleting and lets the configurer's askQuestion group output by primitive.

preserve_user_authored.description: Sentinel. Always `true` for `llm-case` entries so the cleanup engine knows it must drop only rows whose `_meta.generated === true` and rewrite the surrounding JSON in place. Hard-coded contract — subtask 03 must refuse to delete a file when the rule cannot be honoured.
